items.scenario: You are a second-year biology student planning to apply for a research assistant position in a professor's lab next semester. You emailed the lab coordinator last week but have not heard back. You want to confirm your application was received and learn the next steps.
items.direction: Write an email to the lab coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you are writing
Describe your background and interest in the lab
Ask about the selection timeline
items.subject: Following up on my research assistant application

items.scenario: You are enrolled in a literature seminar that requires a final essay due at the end of the month. A family emergency will keep you away from campus for ten days during that period. You want to request more time to complete the assignment.
items.direction: Write an email to your seminar instructor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the situation that has come up
Request additional time for the essay
Suggest a new date you could submit by
items.subject: Request for extension on the seminar essay

items.scenario: You recently received your midterm exam grade in an economics course and believe several answers were marked incorrectly. You reviewed the marking guide and noticed a question where your response matched the expected answer. You want to ask for a review of the grade.
items.direction: Write an email to your economics professor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain which part of the exam you are questioning
Describe why you think the marking was an error
Ask how a grade review can be arranged
items.subject: Question about my midterm exam grade

items.scenario: You are a student worker at the campus writing center and your manager posted next month's schedule. You noticed you were assigned two shifts that conflict with a required lab session. You want to ask whether your shifts can be adjusted.
items.direction: Write an email to the writing center manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Tell the manager which shifts are a problem
Explain the conflict with your lab session
Ask whether the schedule can be changed
items.subject: Scheduling conflict with next month's shifts

items.scenario: You live in a campus apartment and the heating in your unit has not worked properly for the past week. You reported it once by phone but the problem continues, and temperatures are dropping. You want to request a repair as soon as possible.
items.direction: Write an email to the housing maintenance office. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the heating
Explain that an earlier report did not fix it
Ask for the repair to be scheduled soon
items.subject: Heating still not working in my apartment

items.scenario: You are the secretary of your university's photography club, and the club is organizing a weekend trip to a nearby coastal town. A member named Diana emailed asking for details about transportation and what to bring. You want to give her the information and confirm her spot.
items.direction: Write an email to Diana. In your email, do the following:
goals: Tell Diana the travel arrangements for the trip
Explain what she should bring along
Offer to answer any other questions
items.subject: Details for the club's coastal weekend trip

items.scenario: You signed up for a weekend pottery workshop run by the campus arts center, but you just learned the session was moved to a different building without notice. You arrived at the original location and found it empty. You want to share feedback and ask about a makeup option.
items.direction: Write an email to the arts center coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe what happened when you arrived
Explain how the change affected you
Ask whether a makeup session is possible
items.subject: Missed the pottery workshop after the room change

items.scenario: You completed a summer internship at a small publishing company and your supervisor mentioned there might be part-time openings during the school year. You enjoyed the work and would like to be considered for a role. You want to follow up on that conversation.
items.direction: Write an email to your former internship supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain that you are following up on an earlier conversation
Describe your interest in a part-time role
Ask about any current openings
items.subject: Following up about part-time work this term
